trivia: The improved 50-seater sold in the hundreds and carried America's hub-feed traffic for two decades. Passenger fleets are mostly gone, but cargo conversions gave the airframe a busy second act.
If you log one at 3 AM, that is the freighter life: small packages, small airports, tireless airframe.
funFacts: First flight: 1995 (as the CRJ100's re-engined successor).
Over 900 CRJ100/200 series aircraft were built.
Cargo conversions keep large numbers flying today.
